title: At the doctor
setting: You're at the doctor's office, not feeling well.

steps.speaker: Dokter
steps.es: Grüezi, chömed Sie ine. Was händ Sie für Beschwärde?
steps.en: Hello, come in. What's troubling you?

options.es: De Hals tuet weh und ich ha Fieber.
options.en: My throat hurts and I have a fever.
options.feedback: Clear symptom description — exactly what the doctor needs.
True, but vague. Naming the symptoms helps the doctor help you.
Try to describe what you feel — even 'mer isch nöd guet' plus where it hurts.

steps.speaker: Dokter
steps.en: Since when have you had this?

options.feedback: Good — a clear timeframe. 'Öppe' (about) is a handy softener.
Try to estimate — 'sit geschter' or 'sit es paar Täg' helps the diagnosis.

steps.speaker: Dokter
steps.es: Ich lueg mal. Ja, e Erkältig. Nüt Schlimms.
steps.en: Let me look. Yes, a cold. Nothing serious.

options.en: What can I do about it?
options.feedback: Exactly the right follow-up — asking for treatment advice.

options.es: Oh guet, da bin ich erlichteret.
options.feedback: Natural and human. Then you can ask what to do.
Trust the doctor's read — but it's fine to ask what to watch for.

steps.speaker: Dokter
steps.es: Trinked viel, ruehed sich us. I es paar Täg gaht's bsser.
steps.en: Drink lots, rest. In a few days it'll be better.

options.feedback: Shows you'll follow the advice. Nicely done.
For a cold, rest really is the cure — no need for more.
